title: Rahm's Masterclass: Legion XIII Dominates Mexico City
excerpt: Jon Rahm delivered a complete performance to win LIV Golf Mexico City by six shots with a dominant final round 7-under 64, posting four rounds in the 60s.
body:
Rahm Takes Command

Jon Rahm delivered a masterclass at Club de Golf Chapultepec on Sunday, carding a flawless 7-under 64 in the final round to secure a dominant six-shot victory at LIV Golf Mexico City. The Legion XIII captain finished the week at 21 under par, posting four consecutive rounds in the 60s — a performance of rare completeness that left the field in his wake.

Winning Score
21
Victory Margin
6 Shots
Legion XIII Team
45
Rounds in 60s
4

The Masters Lesson Pays Off

Rahm's victory comes just a fortnight after a tense moment at Augusta National with his caddie Adam Hayes. The Legion XIII captain's run to the Masters green jacket was nearly derailed by a putting issue, but a crucial Saturday conversation on the practice range changed everything. "Once it clicked what he was trying to say and what I needed to work on, it was so much better," Rahm recalled. That clarity carried through Mexico City in spectacular fashion — seven birdies without a single bogey on Sunday, and a tee-to-green performance that left opponents powerless.

A Sun Devils Sweep

Behind Rahm, fellow Arizona State products David Puig and Josele Ballester claimed second and third, completing a Sun Devils podium sweep. Puig's blistering third-round 9-under 62 nearly produced a sub-60 score; the Spaniard played six consecutive holes in 7-under and came within striking distance of history. Ballester, meanwhile, led the field in Strokes Gained: Off the Tee (+8.09), showcasing the raw driving talent that has made him one of the season's most dynamic young players.

Team Legion's Historic Week

Legion XIII's dominance extended to the team competition, securing a wire-to-wire victory at 45 under par — nine shots clear of Fireballs GC. It was a statement performance that underlined the depth and cohesion within the Legion XIII camp, with Rahm's individual brilliance boosted by strong supporting performances throughout the week.

The Numbers Tell the Story

Rahm's victory was built on near-flawless tee-to-green golf. He gained +7.62 strokes off the tee (second in the field) and a field-best +10.40 on approach — his most complete week in LIV Golf. Remarkably, he still won by six despite losing just 0.27 strokes on the greens, a testament to the quality of his ball-striking. Louis Oosthuizen led the field in putting (+10.43), whilst young Ballester's driving prowess (+8.09) announced him as one of the tour's most dangerous long-game operators.

Looking Ahead

With Mexico City in the books, LIV Golf heads to Virginia (7–10 May) and then South Korea (28–31 May). For Rahm, the victory marks a clear statement of intent ahead of summer's major championships. For young guns like Ballester and Puig, the podium finishes prove they belong at golf's highest level. The Legion XIII captain has left no doubt: when all systems align, he remains among the best in the world.
